sections.paragraph:
διανοέομαι (s. νοέω, διανόημα, νόημα; Hdt. et al.; Nicol. Dam.: 90 Fgm. 13 p. 339, 24 Jac.; PCairZen 573, 6; Mitt-Wilck. I/2, 1 col. II, 5 [restored]; Herm. Wr. 1, 7; LXX; En 1:2; 2:2; 5:1; Philo; Jos., Vi. 245; Just., D. 72, 1 [in a citation from Esdr., perh. after 2 Esdr 6:21, according to Swete, Introd. 424]) to have in mind, consider GPt 11:44—ὁ Παῦλο[ς τῷ λέοντι καὶ διενοή]θη Παῦλος Paul (was looking) [at the lion and] Paul [noted] (that this was the lion who had come to him and was baptized) AcPl Ha 4, 37.—DELG s.v. νόος.

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

PCairZen PCairZen = Zenon Papyri, Catalogue général . . . du Musée du Caire—List 4

Mitt-Wilck. Mitt-Wilck. = LMitteis/UWilcken, Grundzüge und Chrestomathie der Papyruskunde—Lists 4, 6

col. col. = column

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

perh. perh. = perhaps

Swete Swete = HS., editor of the Greek OT—List 2, beg.

GPt GPt = Gospel of Peter—List 1

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 234
